mission.title: Install and Configure ClickHouse
mission.description: ClickHouse is a real-time analytics database management system designed for generating analytical data reports quickly and efficiently. It is ideal for applications requiring high-performance data processing and analytics. Key trade-offs include the need for careful schema design and potential complexity in managing distributed setups.

steps.title: Add ClickHouse Helm Repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official ClickHouse Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add clickhouse https://github.com/ClickHouse/ClickHouse/tree/v26.2.4.23-stable/charts

```

> This command sets up the repository for installing ClickHouse.

steps.title: Install ClickHouse CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required for ClickHouse to function properly in your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ClickHouse/ClickHouse/v26.2.4.23-stable/charts/clickhouse-operator/crds/clickhouse.altinity.com_clickhouses.yaml

```

> This command installs the ClickHouse CRD.

steps.title: Install ClickHouse Operator
steps.description:
This step installs the ClickHouse operator using Helm, which manages ClickHouse clusters.

```bash

helm install clickhouse-operator clickhouse/clickhouse-operator --namespace clickhouse --create-namespace --version 26.2.4.23

```

> This command deploys the ClickHouse operator in the specified namespace.

steps.title: Create ClickHouse Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a ClickHouse cluster using a Custom Resource (CR) definition.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: clickhouse.altinity.com/v1
kind: ClickHouseCluster
metadata:
  name: example-cluster
  namespace: clickhouse
spec:
  layout: replicated
  shardsCount: 1
  replicasCount: 1
  templates:
    podTemplate:
      spec:
        containers:
          - name: clickhouse
            image: clickhouse/clickhouse-server:latest
EOF

```

> This command creates a simple ClickHouse cluster.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: clickhouse.altinity.com/v1
kind: ClickHouseCluster
metadata:
  name: example-cluster
  namespace: clickhouse
spec:
  layout: replicated

  replicasCount: 1
  templates:
    podTemplate:
      spec:
        containers:
          - name: clickhouse
            image: clickhouse/clickhouse-server:latest
EOF

steps.title: Verify ClickHouse Cluster
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the ClickHouse cluster to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get clickhousecluster -n clickhouse

```

> This command lists the ClickHouse clusters and their statuses.

uninstall.title: Uninstall ClickHouse Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the ClickHouse operator and all associated resources. Be aware that this will lead to data loss if persistent volumes are not retained.

```bash

helm uninstall clickhouse-operator --namespace clickhouse

kubectl delete namespace clickhouse

```

> This command uninstalls the ClickHouse operator.
> This command deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade ClickHouse Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you backup your data. This step upgrades the ClickHouse operator to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets

helm upgrade clickhouse-operator clickhouse/clickhouse-operator --namespace clickhouse --version 26.2.4.23

kubectl uncordon <node-name>

```

> Drain the node to safely upgrade.
> This command upgrades the ClickHouse operator.
> Uncordon the node after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to ClickHouse server'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The ClickHouse server may not be running or is misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the ClickHouse pod using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n clickhouse'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'No resources found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The ClickHouse cluster may not have been created successfully.

**Fix:**
Verify the CR definition and check the status with 'kubectl get clickhousecluster -n clickhouse'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kubernetes cluster may not have enough resources to schedule the ClickHouse pods.

**Fix:**
Ensure your cluster meets the resource requirements (minimum 2 vCPU, 4GB RAM per node).

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The ClickHouse pod may be crashing due to misconfiguration or resource limits.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs and resource limits in the ClickHouse configuration.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new SQL functions and improved replication features.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v25.x, ensure to review the new replication settings.

resolution.summary: You should see the ClickHouse cluster listed with 'kubectl get clickhousecluster -n clickhouse' showing the status as 'Running'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster with sufficient resources and the necessary tools installed.
